data.change_summary: Two amendments addressed: (1) Changed unique_terminal_replay_match return type from Option<usize> to AppResult<Option<usize>> (fs.rs:2834). The contract violation guard (requested_run_id is Some but requested_started_at is None) now returns Err(AppError::CorruptRecord) with a message explicitly identifying the contract violation, instead of returning None which caused callers to emit a misleading 'no matching task run' error. Both call sites updated to propagate the error with ?. (2) Fixed the run_id update path in update_task_run (fs.rs:3167-3198) so that when a single open runless row has a mismatched started_at, the code falls through to the terminal replay matcher instead of immediately returning an error. This enables the partial-write repair path: if the lineage row was already written terminal but the snapshot/journal update failed, replaying the completion with a run_id now finds the completed row by started_at even when a newer retry's open row exists. Added regression test terminal_replay_falls_through_past_mismatched_open_runless_row.
outstanding_risks: Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes.

steps.description: Amendment 1: Changed unique_terminal_replay_match return type from Option<usize> to AppResult<Option<usize>> (fs.rs:2834). The contract violation guard now returns Err(AppError::CorruptRecord) with details 'contract violation: requested_run_id is Some but requested_started_at is None — this would bypass the started_at guard on runless terminal candidates', giving callers a distinct error instead of a misleading 'no matching task run' CorruptRecord.

Amendment 1: Updated both call sites — the named-run terminal fallback (fs.rs:3139) and the runless terminal fallback (fs.rs:3278) — to use match ... ? { Some(index) => index, None => return Err(...) } pattern, propagating the contract violation error distinctly from the 'no matching task run' error.

Amendment 2: Changed the [open_index] if entries[*open_index].run_id.is_none() arm (fs.rs:3167-3198) from immediately returning an error to falling through to the terminal replay matcher. When the open runless row's started_at doesn't match, the code now builds attempt_indices filtered by the requested started_at and calls unique_terminal_replay_match. Only if no terminal match is found does it return an error (with enhanced message noting 'and no terminal row found for the requested started_at').

Added regression test terminal_replay_falls_through_past_mismatched_open_runless_row (service.rs): starts attempt 1 without run_id at t0, completes it, starts attempt 2 (retry) at t1, then replays completion of attempt 1 with run_id='run-late' — must fall through past the open retry row and find the terminal row at t0, backfilling run_id.
Ran cargo test --lib (360 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check (clean).

validation_evidence: cargo test --lib: 360 passed, 0 failed, 0 ignored (was 359 — new terminal_replay_falls_through test added)
cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors

unique_terminal_replay_match contract violation now returns Err(AppError::CorruptRecord { details: 'contract violation: requested_run_id is Some but requested_started_at is None...' }) — distinct from 'no matching task run' errors

terminal_replay_falls_through_past_mismatched_open_runless_row: starts attempt 1 at t0 (no run_id), completes it, starts retry at t1 (no run_id), replays completion of t0 with run_id='run-late' — succeeds, attempt 1 has run_id='run-late' backfilled, attempt 2 still Running with run_id=None

runless_match_rejected_when_started_at_mismatches: existing test still passes — when NO terminal row exists for the requested started_at, the enhanced error message includes 'does not match requested started_at' and 'no terminal row found'

All prior round tests continue to pass: dispatch_v1_snapshot_v2_race_does_not_conflict, named_terminal_replay_succeeds_with_multiple_attempts, terminal_runless_replay_rejected_when_started_at_mismatches, terminal_replay_after_plan_evolution_succeeds, start_replay_after_plan_evolution_does_not_conflict, plan_hash_auto_populated_from_snapshot, explicit_plan_hash_takes_precedence_over_snapshot, runless_start_completed_with_named_run_id, completion_with_conflicting_plan_hash_rejects_runless_match, running_entry_created_before_plan_is_not_backfilled_on_revisit, safe_backfill_fills_plan_hash_when_snapshot_unchanged, safe_backfill_skips_when_snapshot_evolved, terminal_replay_legacy_row_without_plan_hash_stays_unset, terminal_replay_with_provenance_does_not_synthesize_plan_hash, start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render, completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render, journal_merge_fills_all_optional_fields, merge_attempt_entries_fills_all_optional_fields
